steps.title: Find the boundary line equation
steps.content: Reading the graph, the boundary passes through $(0,-6)$ and $(9,0)$. The slope is $\frac{0-(-6)}{9-0}=\frac{2}{3}$, giving $y=\frac{2}{3}x-6$.

steps.title: Pick the inequality direction from the shading
steps.content: The shaded region lies above the boundary and the line is solid, so use $\ge$: $$y\ge \frac{2}{3}x-6.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
